test.name: Initialize project from objective text only — no FamilySearch tree ID
test.description: Single-turn decomposition: the user provides a research objective in detail but no FamilySearch person ID to seed the tree. Tests whether init-project can create a project with a minimal stub person built purely from the objective text. The init-project SKILL.md describes this as the bootstrap-sequence case where `subject_person_ids` may even be null at creation.

input.user_message: Start a new research project. I want to identify the maternal grandmother of Sarah Hennessy, who was born around 1920 in Boston, Massachusetts. Sarah's mother's maiden name was Mary Donovan but I don't know Mary's mother's name. No FamilySearch tree exists for any of these people yet.

additional_criteria: Should create both research.json and tree.gedcomx.json validating against their schemas

Should create at least a minimal stub for Sarah Hennessy in tree.gedcomx.json (name, approximate birth year, place) — and possibly a stub for her mother Mary Donovan. The unknown grandmother does NOT get a stub yet (her name is unknown — creating a placeholder stub would be fabrication)
Should NOT pretend to have data from a FamilySearch tree fixture — there is no person-read or tree fetch in this scenario; the skill must work from objective text alone

Should set `project.subject_person_ids` to the stub IDs that exist, OR to `null` (both are acceptable per research-schema-spec.md §1) — but should NOT include an ID for the unknown grandmother whose stub doesn't exist
